title: U.S. military deploying additional resources to Venezuela as earthquakes death toll mounts
summary: The U.S. military is sending additional resources to Venezuela, as the death toll continues to mount and tens of thousands of people are still believed to be missing following a pair of devastating earthquakes. U.S. South Command (Southcom) announced on Saturday that a specialized rapid response unit was on its way to Venezuela to assist...

title: Letlow wins GOP nod in Louisiana Senate race
summary: Rep. Julia Letlow (R-La.), buoyed by President Trump’s endorsement, is projected to defeat Louisiana State Treasurer John Fleming in the GOP runoff for Sen. Bill Cassidy’s (R-La.) seat, according to Decision Desk HQ. Letlow and Fleming were forced into a runoff after the May 16 primary, which included Cassidy and businessman Mark Spencer, because no single candidate received more...

title: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire
summary: Iran says it has launched retaliatory attacks at US infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain after the US said it hit multiple targets across Iran.

title: US and Iran trade strikes as ceasefire falters
summary: Truce under pressure as Tehran counters US attacks by targeting military installations in Bahrain and Kuwait

title: Rival parties see Burnham’s northern focus as potential weak point
summary: Tory and Reform strategists argue prospective UK PM’s devolution push may turn out to be an electoral liability

title: Why keeping Europe cool need not be a luxury
summary: In the clean energy system the continent is trying to build, air conditioning basically comes for free

title: Spanish import hub urges EU to delay ban on Russian gas
summary: LNG shipments to the bloc have risen in the wake of the Middle East war

title: So, about that saga of Trump’s reflecting pool . . .
summary: In a desperate bid to deflect attention, the president is doing what he loves to do: blaming the libs

title: President Harry Truman’s 1950 Decision to Intervene in Korea
summary: Truman’s decision not to seek congressional authorization for his decision to commit U.S. troops to defend South Korea set a precedent that continues to shape the war powers today.

title: Trump nominates former Oklahoma state trooper to head ICE
summary: President Trump nominated Lance Schroyer, a former Oklahoma state trooper, to direct Immigration and Customs Enforcement. The agency hasn't had a Senate-confirmed director since the Obama administration.

title: Examining Trump's interest in the SAVE America Act
summary: President Trump has an interest in a piece of voting legislation, called the SAVE America Act, that is not shared by all of his Republican colleagues on Capitol Hill.

title: Week in Politics: Trump and bipartisan housing bill, John Bolton; Democratic socialists
summary: President Trump canceled a signing for major housing legislation, former National Security Advisor John Bolton pled guilty to mishandling classified documents, and Democratic Socialists made gains.

title: What would George Washington say? It's a busy year for people who portray him
summary: America's 250th birthday has brought more events and new crowds for Revolutionary reenactors and interpreters. They say Washington's life holds important lessons for our current political divide.

title: Man with same name as U.S. Sen. Dan Sullivan is eligible for Alaska's primary ballot, judge rules
summary: The judge ruled that the division's decision to exclude Dan J. Sullivan because his candidacy was not "in good faith" was not based on the Constitution, Alaska law or the division's own regulations.

title: Trump-backed Julia Letlow faces John Fleming in Louisiana GOP Senate runoff
summary: U.S. Rep. Julia Letlow, who was endorsed by Trump, and state Treasurer John Fleming are competing in the runoff. The two finished ahead of Sen. Bill Cassidy in the May 16 primary after Trump denounced the two-term senator, who voted to convict him following his 2021 impeachment.

title: Trump says he is nominating former Oklahoma state trooper Lance Schroyer as ICE director
summary: Schroyer's nomination comes after former ICE director Todd Lyons resigned at the end of May. David Venturella, a former executive at a private prison operator, has been serving as the acting head of the agency.

title: California state senator running to succeed Pelosi calls out ‘harassment’ at trans march
summary: California state Sen. Scott Wiener (D), who earlier this month advanced to the November general election to succeed former House Speaker Rep. summary: U.S. Central Command (Centcom) said on Saturday that U.S. forces conducted additional strikes against multiple Iranian targets in response to an attack against a Panama-flagged tanker near the Strait of Hormuz. “CENTCOM forces launched strikes today in direct response to continued Iranian aggression against commercial shipping,” Centcom said in a statement on the social platform...

summary: Former NBC News chief political analyst Chuck Todd warned on Friday that President Trump is isolating some of his own party, after the president criticized GOP Sen. Lisa Murkowski (Alaska). “The more Trump goes after Murkowski, the more likely the real number Dems will need to take control of the Senate is ‘3’ not 4...

title: Trump Picks Lance Schroyer as New ICE Director
summary: The president said he was nominating Lance Schroyer, an adviser to Markwayne Mullin, the D.H.S. secretary, to lead the high-profile agency.

title: White House Releases Images of the Trump ‘Patriot Passport’
summary: The State Department said it planned to release 40,000 U.S. passports featuring an image of the president to commemorate the United States’ 250th anniversary.

title: Watergate Today as a Blip of a Story? Vance Might Not Be Entirely Wrong.
summary: Nixon’s offenses were not a deep-state invention. But in the age of Donald Trump, and in a fractured media environment, voters have become more inured to political scandal.

title: Colorado Supreme Court Delay Threatens Democratic Redistricting Effort
summary: Both parties are awaiting a decision on the validity of ballot proposals to redraw Colorado’s maps ahead of 2028. Democrats say each day without a ruling puts the proposals at risk.

title: Jeffries Faces Steep Test as Far Left Builds Strength in Congress
summary: The victories of Darializa Avila Chevalier and other anti-establishment candidates are changing the face of House Democrats, posing a challenge for their leader.

title: GOP candidate with same name as senator can stay on Alaska ballot, judge rules
summary: Supporters of incumbent Republican Sen. Dan Sullivan say a challenger with the same name is not running in “good faith,” but a judge says that is not an issue.

title: Venezuela earthquake death toll rises to 1,430; U.S. to send additional aid
summary: An expected nine-figure monetary aid package comes on top of $150 million that Washington already committed to disaster relief in Venezuela after two massive earthquakes.

title: Trump reveals new images of passport with his portrait to mark America’s 250th
summary: The State Department said the new document “boldly honors” the president and the nation’s founding. The rendering differed from previous images of the passport in several ways.

title: Pro-Israel influencers criticize Vance, but evangelicals remain open to him
summary: Despite attacks from some conservatives, a heavily pro-Israel crowd still has warm words for Vance, with many saying they could pick him as the party’s nominee.

title: Families calling out to loved ones trapped in rubble by Venezuela quakes
summary: In La Guaira, desperate families keep vigil at buildings where they fear their relatives are trapped, but face an impossible task to move heavy debris.

title: In Caracas, this feels like the hardest moment in Venezuela's modern history
summary: Rescue teams are working ceaselessly to reach those trapped under rubble. But as hope fades, anger is growing.

title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
summary: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.

title: Why Kim Jong Un never talks about his mother - or her controversial bloodline
summary: Very few North Koreans know about her, as her origin could threaten the regime’s legitimacy.

title: Identifying Minors Online
summary: Concerns about potential harms to minors on the internet, particularly on social media platforms, have grown in recent years. Dating to the 1990s, policymakers have enacted legislation seeking to protect minors online, some of which create requirements for entities that provide websites, mobile applications, and online platforms (collectively, website operators). Website operators have developed various methods to...

title: What Is Temporary Protected Status?
summary: Temporary Protected Status (TPS) has long been used as a humanitarian solution for migrants who are unable to return home safely. President Trump’s efforts to scale back such programs have further fueled the debate over the immigration policy’s use and scope.

title: Myths, Fables, and Hard Truths About AI Governance
summary: CFR President Michael Froman assesses the U.S. government’s de facto ban on Anthropic’s most powerful AI model and what it reveals about the U.S. regulatory framework for AI.

title: Why Rules-Based Orders Fail
summary: For a brief period after the Cold War, Americans persuaded themselves that the liberal order had become self-sustaining. And yet, any rules-based system elaborate enough to govern and interpret its own operations will eventually confront questions that its rules cannot answer. Originally published at Project Syndicate

title: John F. Kennedy’s “Ich bin ein Berliner” Speech
summary: In just four words, JFK bonded with the people of West Berlin and gave rise to an urban legend.

title: Have Rising Living Standards Pushed Marriage out of Reach?
summary: Have rising material expectations made marriage feel less attainable for today’s young adults? Marriage rates have steadily been declining for several decades. Roughly one-third of Gen Z is expected to remain single by age 45, and to perhaps never marry at all. A common reason young adults give for not being married is a lack of financial resources. title: Children Deserve Education, Not Gender Ideology
summary: From coast to coast, states such as California and New Jersey face no shortage of serious challenges, from the soaring cost of living and rising crime to homelessness and drug abuse. title: Swiss Miss Out on Chance To Save Their Nation From Immigrant Invasion
summary: Snow caps, yes: people caps, no. In a national referendum this week, the people of Switzerland voted against a hard cap of 10 million on the population. Switzerland is a unicorn among the world’s nations: a successful multilingual state. The country was...

title: Remembering Alan Greenspan
summary: The Taylor rule, named after Hoover Institution fellow John Taylor, recommends high interest rates in order to prevent possible inflation. During recessions, it recommends low rates to provide stimulus. The Taylor rule’s main virtues are that it is predictable, and that it can prevent impulsive Fed behavior. If the economy does X, then the Fed will automatically respond with Y.

title: Israeli Ambassador Blasts Lebanon Cease-Fire Talks As A ‘Train Wreck’
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Zohar Palti expressed skepticism about the arrangement — particularly because Israel was not involved in creating it. “It’s not serious,” Palti said of the deconfliction mechanism. “It’s like a wedding without the groom or the bride.”

title: The Inconvenient Truth Meets Inconvenient Facts: 7 Errors In Al Gore’s Documentary
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Bjorn Lomborg said in the Financial Post that an average of 500,000 people died every year due to climate disasters in the 1920s. Today, under 10,000 people die, a decline of 97 per cent.

title: A Democrat-On-Democrat Showdown Is Erupting Over Chicago’s Schools
summary: [Subscription Required] Hoover Institution fellow Michael Hartney said Chicago is following a national trend. Local school-board elections, once low-budget affairs, have increasingly become nationalized, with millions of dollars flowing in.

title: Free Speech Victory For Counselors
summary: Hoover Institution fellow Eugene Volokh submitted a brief supporting neither party in the Chiles v. Salazar case, although the content of what he said seemed to favor Chiles. He emphasized that “speech cannot lose its protection just because it is relabeled conduct and then banned".

title: America’s “direct return” doctrine—and what it means for the world
summary: In Bosnia and Herzegovina, the United States is focused on doing business, at the expense of peace. There are ways for Europeans to acquire a veto over these destabilising activities; but the Trump administration may yet replicate this approach across the globe.

title: British politics ten years after Brexit
summary: Mark Leonard welcomes Marcus Roberts to discuss the legacy of Brexit and the latest developments in British politics

title: Pete Buttigieg and his kids subject to CPS, police investigation after false report
summary: Buttigieg, a potential 2028 Democratic presidential contender, shared his family's experience when police and Child Protective Services came to his home investigating an anonymous tip that was false.

title: DNC plans weekend of local events to focus on affordability concerns under Trump
summary: Two years ago, Democrats were the ones accused of being indifferent to Americans' anger about rising prices. Now they're pointing the finger at Trump, who has downplayed the impact of lingering inflation.

title: Trump's TPS policy is a 'job killer' and bad for Ohio, Gov. DeWine says
summary: A Supreme Court ruling this week cleared the way for the Trump administration to end temporary protected status for Haitians and Syrians. One of the states that could face the biggest impact is Ohio, where more than 10,000 Haitian migrants have settled. Republican Gov. Mike DeWine called the move a mistake. He joined Amna Nawaz to discuss the ruling and what it means for communities in his state.

title: Trump tries out midterms message that focuses on ‘communists’
summary: Trump warned an audience of religious conservatives that communism is spreading in the U.S., a new theme for his midterm campaign.

title: The Marine Mammal Protection Act (P.L. 92522): Primer and Issues for Congress
summary: Congress passed the Marine Mammal Protection Act (MMPA) in 1972 (P.L. 92-522; 16 U.S.C. §§1361-1423h) to conserve marine mammal populations and protect them from extinction or depletion as a result of human activities. Since enactment, Congress has amended the MMPA at times and passed the most recent comprehensive amendments in 1994 (P.L. 103-238). The MMPA intersects with other marine-related legislation, such as...

title: Supply Chain, Energy, and AI Nexus
summary: The authors identify the inventory of critical electrical equipment for artificial intelligence (AI) data center operations and the impact of supply chain vulnerabilities on the U.S. power grid’s ability to meet AI demand by 2030.

title: Can LLM Agents Select and Engage with Biological Tools?
summary: Large language model agents are capable of performing initial interactions with biological tools (BTs), indicating that agents could lower expertise barriers for actors seeking to use BTs. Targeted testing of agent design capabilities is warranted.

title: AI agents put offensive cyber within reach of novices
summary: Agentic AI models now enable non-experts to conduct advanced offensive cyberattacks. This study finds AI agents can autonomously breach complex systems rapidly, highlighting urgent needs for evolved defence and better cyber risk assessments.

title: Forced Labor Contracts Threaten America’s Workplace Freedom
summary: Should the federal government step in and impose union contracts when labor negotiations stall? Rep. Donald Norcross, New Jersey Democrat, thinks so. His Faster Labor Contracts Act (H.R. 5408) passed the House of Representatives on June 9 by a 230-193 vote, with 20 Republicans joining the Democrats in support. Although the legislation aims to accelerate first contracts for newly unionized workplaces, it does so by...

title: From Washington, DC, to the States: Rethinking Head Start
summary: Social science research finds that Head Start, a federally funded preschool program for children from low-income families, does not prepare children for K–12 schools, nor does it improve their social skills. Reports on spending and safety also show that Head Start centers waste money and fail to protect children. After more than six decades and more than $240 billion in federal spending, Head Start is a poor option...

title: Understanding How Impact Fees Shape Housing Development Feasibility and Local Fiscal Revenue
summary: High impact fees contribute meaningfully to housing production costs in California. A 25-percent reduction enables hundreds of new projects in high-cost cities; increased property and sales tax revenue offsets forgone fees in four to seven years.

title: Cultivating a Warrior Ethos in the Air Force
summary: RAND researchers examined how the U.S. Air Force can cultivate an Airminded Warrior identity, finding that the concept requires more development. They recommend several actions to strengthen this identity’s development in initial training and beyond.

title: Beyond Brexit—and back to Europe
summary: Those who follow only intergovernmental negotiations or debates in the media might think that nothing has changed in the decade since the United Kingdom's voters decided to leave the European Union. Yet new polling finds that large majorities on both sides of the English Channel now support re-engagement.

title: Brexit isn’t working: British voters are ready for a European future
summary: Ten years on from the referendum, the British electorate has moved on from its old divisions and—in a world of crisis—is embracing an ambitious move back towards the European Union

title: Peace in the Middle East
summary: Julien Barnes-Dacey welcomes Prince Faisal bin Farhan Al Saud to discuss how the recently inked US-Iran deal could deliver a more stable regional order

summary: On this week’s Unpacking Europe podcast Zselyke Csaky sat down with Milan Nic a senior research fellow at the German Council on Foreign Relations (DGAP) to discuss the latest on EU enlargement. summary: The Department of Defense (DOD), which is “using a secondary Department of War designation,” under Executive Order 14347 dated September 5, 2025, is replacing many elements of the U.S. nuclear command, control, and communications (NC3) architecture as part of the United States’ ongoing efforts to recapitalize its nuclear forces.
